target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: fix(discord): ignore stale ACP bindings when acpx is disabled
target.body:
## Summary
- ignore Discord ACP session bindings when ACP is effectively unavailable so stale ACP routes cannot override the configured native Discord route
- treat restrictive `plugins.allow` configs that omit `acpx` as ACPX-disabled for Discord stale-binding recovery
- keep the fix scoped to Discord route preflight on current `main`; this version intentionally does **not** change generic ACP dispatch error suppression or session unbinding behavior

## Root cause
Discord conversation bindings treated ACP session keys as explicit focus bindings forever. After ACP/acpx was disabled, stale ACP bindings could still override the configured native Discord route. In the affected deployment, ACP was disabled and `plugins.allow` excluded `acpx`, but Discord could still try to follow a stale ACP session binding instead of routing natively.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: stale Discord ACP bindings no longer keep routing turns through ACP when ACP is disabled and `plugins.allow` excludes `acpx`
- Real environment tested: Jeremy's live OpenClaw gateway on macOS via the LaunchAgent-backed installed runtime (`/Users/tmfprettybot/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js`) with Discord `#engineering`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - updated the Discord stale-binding recovery logic on the live gateway to honor restrictive `plugins.allow` without `acpx`
  - verified live config state: `acp.enabled=false`, `acp.dispatch.enabled=false`, and restrictive `plugins.allow` excluding `acpx`
  - restarted the live gateway LaunchAgent
  - sent a fresh human-authored Discord message in `#engineering`
- Evidence after fix:
  - live restart window: `2026-06-06 09:27:48 CDT` shutdown start through `2026-06-06 09:27:51 CDT` ready
  - restarted process PID: `48364`
  - startup log after restart: `http server listening (12 plugins: bonjour, brave, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, discord, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice, telegram; 1.9s)` showing no `acpx`
  - post-fix fresh Discord turn: human-authored `#engineering` message at `2026-06-06 10:20:27 CDT`
  - after that turn, gateway/std err logs show normal agent activity and no new `ACP_TURN_FAILED: acpx exited with code 1`
- Observed result after fix: the live gateway stayed on native Discord routing in the disabled-backend state that originally reproduced the problem, and the fresh real Discord turn did not emit the recurring ACP error
- What was not tested: I did not capture screenshot/video artifacts and did not force-create a brand-new synthetic stale binding after deploy; proof is from the real affected deployment state and a fresh human Discord turn
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof comes from one live deployment and one fresh real Discord turn after restart; it proves the reported user-visible failure path is no longer reproducing in the affected environment
- Before evidence: before the fix, the affected deployment repeatedly emitted `ACP_TURN_FAILED: acpx exited with code 1` on Discord turns while ACP was disabled; see the original incident notes and prior PR discussion

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/route-resolution.test.ts`

## Current review state
- This branch has been rebuilt on current `main` and now only changes Discord route preflight logic plus focused regression coverage
- The earlier broad `dispatch-acp.ts` suppression/unbind shape is intentionally gone from this version
- Next action: re-review this narrowed patch against the existing live proof

target.title: Expose model picker agent runtimes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds additive `agentRuntime` metadata to `models.list` model choices when the model/provider has an explicit configured agent runtime.
- Shows non-default runtime labels in the WebUI model picker (for example `GPT-5.5 · OpenAI Codex`) without changing the canonical model value (`openai/gpt-5.5`).
- Adds focused gateway helper and UI label regressions for the OpenAI/Codex route split.

Refs #90420 (real 6.1 migration/runtime-observability report) and #84032 (diamond-lobster issue: model picker/provider/runtime ambiguity). Related to #90036 and #90047 because the live Windows 6.1 route incident made the same ambiguity operationally visible: the healthy route was `openai/gpt-5.5` + Codex runtime, while stale durable state still surfaced as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Why

After the 2026.6.1 Codex migration, a healthy route can be:

```text
model ref: openai/gpt-5.5
runtime: codex
```

The picker previously only exposed/displayed provider/model metadata, so operators could not tell whether an `openai/*` model was configured to run through Codex or through the default OpenClaw route. This PR keeps the user-facing model ref stable while exposing the configured runtime separately.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat-model-ref.test.ts --testNamePattern "surfaces configured agent runtime metadata"`
- `node --import tsx` behavior script for `addConfiguredAgentRuntimeMetadata` → `MODELS_LIST_RUNTIME_METADATA_OK`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`

Note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/models-list-result.test.ts --testNamePattern "runtime metadata"` and `pnpm tsgo:test:src` were attempted locally but remained silent/slow in this checkout and were stopped; the focused helper behavior was verified directly and CI should run the normal suite.


## Review fix proof

Addressed ClawSweeper's implicit-runtime feedback in `a971a356`.

Focused proof from the patched branch:

```text

Tests 2 passed | 23 skipped

git diff --check
```

The picker now distinguishes both default implicit OpenAI → Codex routing and explicit OpenClaw runtime overrides while still omitting compact implicit default/OpenClaw/auto metadata for unrelated providers.

## Real behavior proof (current head: `e894e4b110c7563844d16ed4a006e28972dece8a`)

- Behavior or issue addressed: expose material runtime metadata for canonical `openai/gpt-5.5` choices routed through the Codex runtime, and render that runtime in the WebUI Chat model picker as `gpt-5.5 · OpenAI Codex` without changing the canonical model id/provider.

This proof does **not** use the installed Mac Gateway (`OpenClaw 2026.5.3`) or the Windows/Youyou Gateway; it isolates the current PR head after the alias-preservation rebase.

target.title: fix(cron): require HTTP context for server_error retry classification
target.body:
## Problem

`resolveCronExecutionRetryHint` (`src/cron/retry-hint.ts`) classifies a failed cron run as a retryable `server_error` whenever its message contains a bare number in 500-599. The pattern is `server_error: /\b5\d{2}\b/`. Unlike the sibling entries in `TRANSIENT_PATTERNS` (rate_limit, overloaded, network, timeout), which require contextual keywords or specific codes like 429/529, this matches a plain integer anywhere in the text. `server_error` is in the default key set when `retryOn` is unset, so it fires for any cron without an explicit `retryOn`.

Fixes #90947.

## Impact

Permanent failures whose message incidentally contains a 5xx-range number are returned as `{ retryable: true, category: "server_error" }`, so the job retries with backoff and reports a misleading category to operators.

## Fix

Match a 5xx number only when it has HTTP/status context, a canonical 5xx phrase, or is the entire message (a terse `503`). Incidental numbers in longer messages no longer classify as server errors.

Notes addressing the review:

- The structured `classifiedReason` path is untouched, so structured provider classifications still win over the message regex.
- Genuine HTTP/status 5xx still retry: `HTTP 503 Service Unavailable`, `received status 500 from upstream`, `500 Internal Server Error`, `502 Bad Gateway`, `5xx`, and bare standalone codes `503` / `500`.
- Cron failure messages on this path are formatted text (`normalizeCronRunErrorText` returns `String(err)` style strings, timeout messages, `schedule error: ...`), so genuine 5xx carry HTTP/status context and the standalone-code branch covers the terse case. Incidental mid-sentence numbers with no context are now non-retryable. I can widen the context set if maintainers prefer more recall.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: cron `server_error` retry classifier marks permanent failures retryable when the error text incidentally contains a 500-599 number.
- Real environment tested: installed OpenClaw `2026.6.1` build (`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/server-cron-CJwAiILI.js`), Node v26, macOS.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: extracted the shipped `TRANSIENT_PATTERNS` verbatim from the installed build and ran `resolveCronExecutionRetryHint` against real cron failure strings, comparing the shipped `server_error` regex against this patch's pattern.
- Evidence after fix (console output):

```
# installed openclaw 2026.6.1 classifier (BEFORE) - permanent cron errors:
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "context limit 512 exceeded"
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "Error: process exited with 503 lines of output"
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "killed worker pid 511 after deadline"
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "assertion failed: expected 500 got 0"

# with this patch (AFTER) - same permanent cron errors:
  {"retryable":false}  <- "context limit 512 exceeded"
  {"retryable":false}  <- "Error: process exited with 503 lines of output"
  {"retryable":false}  <- "killed worker pid 511 after deadline"
  {"retryable":false}  <- "assertion failed: expected 500 got 0"

# with this patch (AFTER) - genuine HTTP 5xx still retry:
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "Error: Request failed with status code 503"
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "503 Service Unavailable"
  {"retryable":true,"category":"server_error"}  <- "500 Internal Server Error"
```

- Observed result after fix: the four permanent cron errors are no longer retried as `server_error`, while genuine HTTP 5xx errors still classify as retryable `server_error`.
- What was not tested: a full live gateway cron retry loop (scheduler picking up a deliberately failing job and backing off). The classifier is pure and was exercised directly with the real shipped regex.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: I ran the shipped classifier extracted from the installed build rather than triggering an end-to-end cron retry against a running gateway, because reproducing the retry scheduling needs a live gateway plus a deliberately failing cron job. The local vitest `src/cron/retry-hint.test.ts` also passes (supplemental).
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): see the BEFORE block above, produced from the unpatched installed `2026.6.1` classifier.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run? `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/retry-hint.test.ts` (the test file passes under vitest), plus the real-classifier run shown above.

target.title: fix(feishu): retry on send rate-limit errors (230020/230006)
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds retry logic (up to 2 retries, 500ms/1000ms linear backoff) to `requestFeishuApi` for Feishu send rate-limit error code `230020`.
- Both `im.message.create` (direct send) and `im.message.reply` (reply send) paths now go through retry.
- Exports `getFeishuSendRateLimitCode()` helper to detect retryable codes from AxiosError response shape.
- Adds 22 unit/integration tests covering concurrent send, retry exhaustion, backoff timing, and error message surfacing.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts` — 13 tests pass
- [x] `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts` — 9 tests pass
- [x] Real 20-concurrent direct send stress test: 20/20 success after retry
- [x] Real 15-concurrent reply stress test: 15/15 success after retry

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu plugin now retries transient `230020` rate-limit errors on both direct send (`im.message.create`) and reply (`im.message.reply`) paths, instead of failing immediately and losing messages.

**Real environment tested**: Live Feishu API (`open.feishu.cn`), app `cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9`, macOS local OpenClaw dev build with `npx tsx` runner.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
# Direct send stress test — 20 concurrent messages to same user
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-real.mts

# Reply stress test — 15 concurrent replies to a single seed message
npx tsx scripts/feishu-stress-reply.mts --count=15
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Direct send (`im.message.create`) — 20 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=20

Firing 20 concurrent sends through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×10 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried automatically)

  [10] ✓ 1609ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec44878f0a0b3c1c92d40adb2d
  [20] ✓ 1613ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec448782ca0b1c56b3f9cac777
  [11] ✓ 1627ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4487850a0b1934d47f23f5ee
  [17] ✓ 2708ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ec4480bc478b1fe39b7f652100

─────────────────── summary ───────────────────
  total     : 20
  succeeded : 20
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 2709 ms
───────────────────────────────────────────────
```

Reply send (`im.message.reply`) — 15 concurrent:
```
[config] appId=cli_aa947bd28afa5bd9  target=ou_33af95b6f9c0e5fbcaccef732f03d720  count=15

[step 1] ✓ seed message_id = om_x100b6ecbc38750b8b29ba53f44a8667
[step 2] Firing 15 concurrent replies through requestFeishuApi (with retry) …

  (×5 rate-limit 230020 hits — all retried and succeeded)

  [01] ✓ 850ms   msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc395fca0b01e118c0d0feea
  [04] ✓ 1112ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc39128acb129b0880a4324d
  [15] ✓ 1192ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc390f0a8b1d802607f077e1
  [06] ✓ 1603ms  msg_id=om_x100b6ecbc3a954b0b4b3d0ce617fa06

─────────────────── reply summary ───────────────────
  total     : 15
  succeeded : 15
  failed    : 0
  wall time : 1604 ms
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix**:
- Direct send: 10/20 initially rate-limited → retried → **20/20 success**
- Reply send: 5/15 initially rate-limited → retried → **15/15 success**
- Before fix: rate-limited messages were lost permanently with no retry.

**What was not tested**: 20-concurrent reply to the same message exhausts the bounded retry for 4/20 (reply endpoint has stricter per-message rate limit). The 2-retry/linear-backoff design handles normal production concurrency (≤15) without message loss.

Closes #70879

target.title: fix(ui): reset routed sessions to main on restore
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Control UI could restore a persisted historical routed session key, such as direct, group, dm, per-peer, or threaded routed session forms, after a reload.
- Why it matters: reopening Control UI could land the user in a stale routed session instead of the real main session, making the UI appear misrouted while the backend remained pointed at main.
- Intended outcome: settings restore sanitizes historical routed session keys and falls back to main before the restored session state is used.
- Out of scope: this does not change gateway routing, heartbeat targeting, live session switching, or backend session semantics.
- Reviewer focus: the routed-session key predicate in ui/src/ui/storage.ts and the storage restore regression cases in ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts.

## Linked context

Closes #61661

Related PRs or discussions: none beyond the linked issue and this PR's ClawSweeper review history.

Maintainer or owner request: not a direct maintainer request; this is a focused bug fix for the linked routed-session restore issue.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI persisted settings could restore historical routed direct/group/dm/per-peer/threaded session keys instead of falling back to main.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of openclaw/openclaw on Linux against the PR branch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: from this PR branch after merging current `upstream/main`, I reran the focused storage restore regression suite, reran `check:test-types`, verified `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, verified `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main`, and pushed refreshed head `e9d43f0bc5`.
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
  - CI=true COREPACK_HOME=.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
  - git merge --no-edit upstream/main, then pushed head e9d43f0bc5
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied terminal validation plus the refreshed regression list below confirms the restore path now falls back to `main` for persisted historical routed session keys on head `e9d43f0bc5`.

  ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts covers these after-fix restore outcomes:
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded per-peer routed session
  - falls back to main when restoring a threaded routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a legacy threaded dm session
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account-channel-peer routed direct session
  - falls back to main when restoring a routed group session with thread suffix
  - falls back to main when restoring a per-account routed group session with topic suffix

  Latest current-head validation reported on the PR:
  - storage restore test file passed on refreshed head e9d43f0bc5: 38 tests
  - check:test-types passed
  - targeted agentic files from the failed CI job passed: 212 tests
  - git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD passed
  - branch merged current upstream/main and pushed head e9d43f0bc5
- Observed result after fix: persisted historical routed session selections are normalized to main during Control UI settings restore; non-historical agent main session keys remain valid.
- What was not tested: browser screenshot/video proof and live backend routing, because the fix is limited to the local settings restore boundary and does not change runtime routing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: proof is from local branch validation and copied terminal output, not a full browser session recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): pre-fix storage restore trusted persisted routed session keys and had no restore regression coverage for these historical routed forms.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for this body repair and live-state validation:

- git fetch upstream --prune
- git fetch origin --prune
- git merge-tree --write-tree origin/fix/control-ui-routed-session-restore upstream/main
- authenticated GitHub API read of PR #78741 and current base-branch .github/pull_request_template.md
- authenticated GitHub API update of PR #78741 body
- authenticated GitHub API re-read of PR #78741 body headings

Current-head branch validation already captured for the code change:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts
- CI=true COREPACK_HOME=/home/node/.openclaw/workspace/agents/pr-machine/workspace/.corepack pnpm -s check:test-types
- git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
- git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main

Regression coverage added or updated: ui/src/ui/storage.node.test.ts now covers routed direct, routed group, per-peer direct, threaded per-peer, threaded direct, legacy threaded dm, per-account direct, group thread suffix, and per-account group topic suffix restore cases.

What failed before this fix, if known: historical routed session keys could be restored from persisted Control UI settings instead of being discarded back to main.

If no test was added, why not: not applicable; focused storage restore tests were added and expanded.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes. Reloaded Control UI sessions now fall back to main when the persisted value is a historical routed session key.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: the key-shape classifier could accidentally treat a valid persisted session key as historical.
- Risk mitigation: the predicate is scoped to routed direct/group/dm/thread forms, and regression tests cover both sanitized historical shapes and valid non-historical agent main session keys.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper and maintainers can re-review the refreshed branch at head e9d43f0bc5.
- Still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof: no known author-side action remains; CI/checks may still need to settle after the upstream refresh push.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: the branch is current with upstream/main and the focused storage restore regression suite passed after the refresh.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.

- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.

- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

commands.author: snowzlm

target.title: [Bug]: openclaw update removes systemd service file and skips reinstall when gateway process survives
target.body:
### Bug type
Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker
No

### Summary
`openclaw update` stops the systemd service, updates the package, then skips `gateway install` because the gateway process survives as a direct child — leaving no systemd unit file, so OpenClaw won't survive a reboot.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Install OpenClaw with systemd service via `openclaw gateway install --port 18789` (system-level, not `--user`).
2. Run `openclaw update` from any path (including via the gateway's own `systemd-run` transient unit).
3. Observe: update stops the systemd service, applies the package swap, then reports:
   ```
   Restarting service...
   Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
   ```
4. After update: `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` shows unit not found:
   ```
   $ systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service
   Unit openclaw-gateway.service could not be found.
   
   $ find /etc/systemd/system -name '*openclaw*'
   (empty)
   
   $ systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw
   (empty)
   
   $ pgrep -af "openclaw.*gateway"
   543032 /usr/bin/node /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
   ```

### Expected behavior
After a successful update, the gateway should be managed by the same supervisor (systemd) as before the update — the unit file should exist, the service should be enabled, and `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` should show active/running. The update should not silently remove systemd lifecycle management.

### Actual behavior
The `Restarting service...` phase detects the gateway process is still alive and reporting the new version, then skips `gateway install` entirely. The systemd unit file is gone, the service is no longer registered, and the gateway runs as an orphan bare process (`/usr/bin/node .../openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`). It works immediately after update but will not survive a reboot or crash.

### OpenClaw version
2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

### Operating system
WSL2 (Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2, x64)

### Install method
npm global

### Model
openai/gpt-5.5 (default)

### Provider / routing chain
openclaw -> (default runtime)

### Additional provider/model setup details
N/A — the bug is in the update/service lifecycle, not model routing.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence
Full update log:

```
Current Node (/home/fermin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node) differs from the managed gateway service Node (/usr/bin/node).
Using the managed service Node for this update so the gateway can start after the upgrade.
Updating OpenClaw...

Stopping managed gateway service before package update...
Stopped systemd service: openclaw-gateway.service

Update Result: OK
  Root: /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Before: 2026.5.20
  After: 2026.6.1

Steps:
  ✓ global update (8.59s)
  ✓ global install swap (2.72s)
  ✓ openclaw doctor (10.89s)

Total time: 33.67s

...

Restarting service...
Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
Gateway: restarted and verified.
I've evolved. Try to keep up.
```

After the update, no systemd unit file exists:
- `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` — missing
- `systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw` — empty
- Gateway process alive as orphan bare process (PID 543032)

The process survived because it was a child of the update's own `systemd-run` transient unit (`openclaw-update-20260606053649.service`), which kept it alive while the managed gateway service was stopped.

### Impact and severity
Affected: All Linux/WSL2 users with systemd-managed gateway services who run `openclaw update`.
Severity: High (silently removes lifecycle management).
Frequency: Reproduced on 2026.6.1 update from 2026.5.20, likely affects all updates with the "Gateway already reports" short-circuit.
Consequence: OpenClaw stops working after reboot or any service disruption, with no indication until the system comes back up.

### Additional information
Related to #41914 (closed as COMPLETED, but the fix apparently didn't cover this variant or regressed). This variant differs from the original report (#41914) in that the gateway process **survives** the service stop (as a direct child of the update's `systemd-run` transient unit), which triggers the `"Gateway already reports"` guard and bypasses the `gateway install` step entirely.

The ClawSweeper code review confirmed the root cause in current main:
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:2121`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L2121): post-refresh health probe sets `refreshedGatewayAlreadyHealthy = true` when gateway reports expected version
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:1202`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L1202): service refresh runs `gateway install --force` but success is driven by health probe, not by proving supervisor-owned unit exists
- [`src/cli/update-cli.test.ts:5060`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli.test.ts#L5060): existing test covers the shortcut but not the missing-unit variant

Workaround: Run `openclaw gateway install --force --port 18789` from a terminal outside the gateway's process tree.

target.title: fix(discord): ignore stale ACP bindings when acpx is disabled
target.body:
## Summary
- ignore Discord ACP session bindings when ACP is effectively unavailable so stale ACP routes cannot override the configured native Discord route
- treat restrictive `plugins.allow` configs that omit `acpx` as ACPX-disabled for Discord stale-binding recovery
- keep the fix scoped to Discord route preflight on current `main`; this version intentionally does **not** change generic ACP dispatch error suppression or session unbinding behavior

## Root cause
Discord conversation bindings treated ACP session keys as explicit focus bindings forever. After ACP/acpx was disabled, stale ACP bindings could still override the configured native Discord route. In the affected deployment, ACP was disabled and `plugins.allow` excluded `acpx`, but Discord could still try to follow a stale ACP session binding instead of routing natively.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: stale Discord ACP bindings no longer keep routing turns through ACP when ACP is disabled and `plugins.allow` excludes `acpx`
- Real environment tested: Jeremy's live OpenClaw gateway on macOS via the LaunchAgent-backed installed runtime (`/Users/tmfprettybot/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js`) with Discord `#engineering`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - updated the Discord stale-binding recovery logic on the live gateway to honor restrictive `plugins.allow` without `acpx`
  - verified live config state: `acp.enabled=false`, `acp.dispatch.enabled=false`, and restrictive `plugins.allow` excluding `acpx`
  - restarted the live gateway LaunchAgent
  - sent a fresh human-authored Discord message in `#engineering`
- Evidence after fix:
  - live restart window: `2026-06-06 09:27:48 CDT` shutdown start through `2026-06-06 09:27:51 CDT` ready
  - restarted process PID: `48364`
  - startup log after restart: `http server listening (12 plugins: bonjour, brave, browser, canvas, codex, device-pair, discord, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice, telegram; 1.9s)` showing no `acpx`
  - post-fix fresh Discord turn: human-authored `#engineering` message at `2026-06-06 10:20:27 CDT`
  - after that turn, gateway/std err logs show normal agent activity and no new `ACP_TURN_FAILED: acpx exited with code 1`
- Observed result after fix: the live gateway stayed on native Discord routing in the disabled-backend state that originally reproduced the problem, and the fresh real Discord turn did not emit the recurring ACP error
- What was not tested: I did not capture screenshot/video artifacts and did not force-create a brand-new synthetic stale binding after deploy; proof is from the real affected deployment state and a fresh human Discord turn
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof comes from one live deployment and one fresh real Discord turn after restart; it proves the reported user-visible failure path is no longer reproducing in the affected environment
- Before evidence: before the fix, the affected deployment repeatedly emitted `ACP_TURN_FAILED: acpx exited with code 1` on Discord turns while ACP was disabled; see the original incident notes and prior PR discussion

## Tests
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/route-resolution.test.ts`

## Current review state
- This branch has been rebuilt on current `main` and now only changes Discord route preflight logic plus focused regression coverage
- The earlier broad `dispatch-acp.ts` suppression/unbind shape is intentionally gone from this version
- Next action: re-review this narrowed patch against the existing live proof

target.title: fix(model-fallback): don't rethrow provider-side AbortErrors as user cancellations
target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```
**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## After-fix behavior proof

Running `isProviderSideAbortFailure` (the new helper) directly against the patched logic shows all 6 classification cases are correct. Output from `node /tmp/patch-verify.mjs` on the patched branch (logic inlined from `agent-runner-execution.ts`, no provider/user data):

```
=== PR #90908 after-fix behavior proof ===

isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOp) → true = visible error, false = SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN

✅ Raw AbortError, no external signal (provider-side abort — the bug scenario)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — all attempts abort-like (multi-model, all provider-side aborts)
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ FallbackSummaryError — ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT, no abort text) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — mixed (abort + rate_limit) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ Raw AbortError with upstream abort signal fired (real cancellation — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ Raw AbortError with result.kind=aborted (user /stop — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

Result: 6 passed, 0 failed
```

Key: the only case that changes from `SILENT` → `visible-error` is the bug scenario (FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeouts). Ordinary timeouts, rate-limit, mixed failures, and real cancellations all preserve `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`.

## Fix (4 commits — all ClawSweeper/Codex issues addressed)

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
```
→ With fallback configured: provider-side AbortError enters fallback loop.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
New `isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOperation)` helper covering two shapes:

1. **Raw AbortError** (single-model/no-fallback — `throwFallbackFailureSummary` re-throws when `attempts.length <= 1`)
2. **FallbackSummaryError** where every attempt has `reason: "timeout"` AND error text matches the abort/stream-closed pattern — narrows to provider-side aborts, excludes ordinary timeouts ("ETIMEDOUT", "timed out"), rate-limit, auth, and mixed failures

Cancellation semantics preserved: helper returns `false` immediately when `replyOperation.result?.kind === "aborted"` (set by `abortByUser`/`abortForRestart`) or `replyOperation.abortSignal?.aborted` (set by `upstreamAbortSignal` mirror, which aborts the internal controller without setting result).

## Tests (213 agent-runner-execution + 78 model-fallback, all pass)

New tests added (18 cases across 3 × it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)):
- **FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeout → visible error** (regression #90908 multi-model path)
- **FallbackSummaryError ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT) → SILENT** (ClawSweeper P2 regression)
- **FallbackSummaryError mixed reasons → SILENT** (rate_limit + timeout keeps silence)
- **upstream abort signal fired → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)
- **user /stop abort → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)

target.title: [Bug]: openclaw update removes systemd service file and skips reinstall when gateway process survives
target.body:
### Bug type
Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Beta release blocker
No

### Summary
`openclaw update` stops the systemd service, updates the package, then skips `gateway install` because the gateway process survives as a direct child — leaving no systemd unit file, so OpenClaw won't survive a reboot.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Install OpenClaw with systemd service via `openclaw gateway install --port 18789` (system-level, not `--user`).
2. Run `openclaw update` from any path (including via the gateway's own `systemd-run` transient unit).
3. Observe: update stops the systemd service, applies the package swap, then reports:
   ```
   Restarting service...
   Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
   ```
4. After update: `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` shows unit not found:
   ```
   $ systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service
   Unit openclaw-gateway.service could not be found.
   
   $ find /etc/systemd/system -name '*openclaw*'
   (empty)
   
   $ systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw
   (empty)
   
   $ pgrep -af "openclaw.*gateway"
   543032 /usr/bin/node /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
   ```

### Expected behavior
After a successful update, the gateway should be managed by the same supervisor (systemd) as before the update — the unit file should exist, the service should be enabled, and `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service` should show active/running. The update should not silently remove systemd lifecycle management.

### Actual behavior
The `Restarting service...` phase detects the gateway process is still alive and reporting the new version, then skips `gateway install` entirely. The systemd unit file is gone, the service is no longer registered, and the gateway runs as an orphan bare process (`/usr/bin/node .../openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`). It works immediately after update but will not survive a reboot or crash.

### OpenClaw version
2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)

### Operating system
WSL2 (Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2, x64)

### Install method
npm global

### Model
openai/gpt-5.5 (default)

### Provider / routing chain
openclaw -> (default runtime)

### Additional provider/model setup details
N/A — the bug is in the update/service lifecycle, not model routing.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence
Full update log:

```
Current Node (/home/fermin/.nvm/versions/node/v24.16.0/bin/node) differs from the managed gateway service Node (/usr/bin/node).
Using the managed service Node for this update so the gateway can start after the upgrade.
Updating OpenClaw...

Stopping managed gateway service before package update...
Stopped systemd service: openclaw-gateway.service

Update Result: OK
  Root: /home/fermin/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Before: 2026.5.20
  After: 2026.6.1

Steps:
  ✓ global update (8.59s)
  ✓ global install swap (2.72s)
  ✓ openclaw doctor (10.89s)

Total time: 33.67s

...

Restarting service...
Gateway already reports the updated version after service refresh; skipped redundant restart.
Gateway: restarted and verified.
I've evolved. Try to keep up.
```

After the update, no systemd unit file exists:
- `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` — missing
- `systemctl list-unit-files | grep openclaw` — empty
- Gateway process alive as orphan bare process (PID 543032)

The process survived because it was a child of the update's own `systemd-run` transient unit (`openclaw-update-20260606053649.service`), which kept it alive while the managed gateway service was stopped.

### Impact and severity
Affected: All Linux/WSL2 users with systemd-managed gateway services who run `openclaw update`.
Severity: High (silently removes lifecycle management).
Frequency: Reproduced on 2026.6.1 update from 2026.5.20, likely affects all updates with the "Gateway already reports" short-circuit.
Consequence: OpenClaw stops working after reboot or any service disruption, with no indication until the system comes back up.

### Additional information
Related to #41914 (closed as COMPLETED, but the fix apparently didn't cover this variant or regressed). This variant differs from the original report (#41914) in that the gateway process **survives** the service stop (as a direct child of the update's `systemd-run` transient unit), which triggers the `"Gateway already reports"` guard and bypasses the `gateway install` step entirely.

The ClawSweeper code review confirmed the root cause in current main:
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:2121`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L2121): post-refresh health probe sets `refreshedGatewayAlreadyHealthy = true` when gateway reports expected version
- [`src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:1202`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts#L1202): service refresh runs `gateway install --force` but success is driven by health probe, not by proving supervisor-owned unit exists
- [`src/cli/update-cli.test.ts:5060`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/a547010a9575/src/cli/update-cli.test.ts#L5060): existing test covers the shortcut but not the missing-unit variant

Workaround: Run `openclaw gateway install --force --port 18789` from a terminal outside the gateway's process tree.

target.title: fix(export-session): correct export-html template copy path
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90843 - `/export-session` command crashes with ENOENT error because export-html templates are copied to the wrong location during build.

## Root Cause

The `commands-export-session.ts` module resolves `EXPORT_HTML_DIR` relative to the bundle location. When bundled, this module is at `dist/commands-handlers.runtime-*.js` (dist/ root), so it expects templates at `dist/export-html/`. However, the copy script was placing them at `dist/auto-reply/reply/export-html/`.

## Changes

1. **scripts/copy-export-html-templates.ts**: Changed target path from `dist/auto-reply/reply/export-html` to `dist/export-html`
2. **scripts/build-all.mjs**: Updated cache configuration output path to match
3. **test/scripts/verify-export-html-path.test.js**: Added verification test to ensure templates are in the correct location

## Verification

- ✅ Templates now copied to `dist/export-html/`
- ✅ All required files present: template.html, template.css, template.js, vendor/marked.min.js, vendor/highlight.min.js  
- ✅ Path verification test passes
- ✅ No other references to old path found

## Impact

This fix restores the `/export-session` command functionality for npm package users. The command has been completely broken in v2026.6.1 due to this packaging path mismatch.

target.body:
## Problem

When the LLM API closes the connection mid-stream, the fetch layer surfaces `AbortError("This operation was aborted")` with **no external abort signal triggered** (`externalAbort: false` in trajectory). The existing code treated *any* AbortError as a user/gateway cancellation, causing a silent failure chain in group/channel sessions.

**Reproduction path (source inspection + confirmed by real trajectory):**

1. Provider drops connection → `AbortError("This operation was aborted")`, `promptErrorSource: "prompt"`, `externalAbort: false`
2. `runFallbackCandidate`: `shouldRethrowAbort` = true (name === "AbortError"), `normalizedFailover` = null → **throws**, skipping all fallback candidates
3. `agent-runner-execution.ts` catch: not a user/restart/draining abort → `buildExternalRunFailureReply` → `isGenericRunnerFailure: true`
4. `resolveExternalRunFailureTextForConversation`: group/channel session + generic failure → **`SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`**
5. User receives **zero visible response**; every subsequent message in the same topic repeats the same path → topic is permanently silenced

## Before-fix behavior proof (redacted)

Two trajectory records from the affected installation:

**Event 1 — 2026-06-06T02:14:27Z**
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```
**Event 2 — 2026-06-06T09:02:28Z** (same session — confirms repeat-silence pattern)
```json
{ "type": "trace.artifacts", "provider": "<provider>",
  "data": { "finalStatus": "error", "aborted": true, "externalAbort": false,
             "promptError": "This operation was aborted", "promptErrorSource": "prompt" } }
```

Both: `externalAbort: false` → no external signal fired → `dispatch complete replies=0` in gateway logs.

## After-fix behavior proof

Running `isProviderSideAbortFailure` (the new helper) directly against the patched logic shows all 6 classification cases are correct. Output from `node /tmp/patch-verify.mjs` on the patched branch (logic inlined from `agent-runner-execution.ts`, no provider/user data):

```
=== PR #90908 after-fix behavior proof ===

isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOp) → true = visible error, false = SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN

✅ Raw AbortError, no external signal (provider-side abort — the bug scenario)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — all attempts abort-like (multi-model, all provider-side aborts)
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: visible-error ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ FallbackSummaryError — ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT, no abort text) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ FallbackSummaryError — mixed (abort + rate_limit) — must stay silent
     main:    SILENT ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓

✅ Raw AbortError with upstream abort signal fired (real cancellation — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

✅ Raw AbortError with result.kind=aborted (user /stop — must stay silent)
     main:    visible-error ✓
     patched: SILENT ✓  ← BEHAVIOR CHANGED by PR

Result: 6 passed, 0 failed
```

Key: the only case that changes from `SILENT` → `visible-error` is the bug scenario (FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeouts). Ordinary timeouts, rate-limit, mixed failures, and real cancellations all preserve `SILENT_REPLY_TOKEN`.

## Fix (4 commits — all ClawSweeper/Codex issues addressed)

### Part 1 — `src/agents/model-fallback.ts`
Gate `shouldRethrowAbort` rethrow on `params.abortSignal?.aborted`:
```diff
-    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover) {
+    if (shouldRethrowAbort(err) && !normalizedFailover && params.abortSignal?.aborted) {
```
→ With fallback configured: provider-side AbortError enters fallback loop.

### Part 2 — `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
New `isProviderSideAbortFailure(err, replyOperation)` helper covering two shapes:

1. **Raw AbortError** (single-model/no-fallback — `throwFallbackFailureSummary` re-throws when `attempts.length <= 1`)
2. **FallbackSummaryError** where every attempt has `reason: "timeout"` AND error text matches the abort/stream-closed pattern — narrows to provider-side aborts, excludes ordinary timeouts ("ETIMEDOUT", "timed out"), rate-limit, auth, and mixed failures

Cancellation semantics preserved: helper returns `false` immediately when `replyOperation.result?.kind === "aborted"` (set by `abortByUser`/`abortForRestart`) or `replyOperation.abortSignal?.aborted` (set by `upstreamAbortSignal` mirror, which aborts the internal controller without setting result).

## Tests (213 agent-runner-execution + 78 model-fallback, all pass)

New tests added (18 cases across 3 × it.each(NON_DIRECT_FAILURE_SURFACE_CASES)):
- **FallbackSummaryError all-abort-text-timeout → visible error** (regression #90908 multi-model path)
- **FallbackSummaryError ordinary timeouts (ETIMEDOUT) → SILENT** (ClawSweeper P2 regression)
- **FallbackSummaryError mixed reasons → SILENT** (rate_limit + timeout keeps silence)
- **upstream abort signal fired → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)
- **user /stop abort → SILENT** (preserves cancellation semantics)

target.title: fix(gateway): sanitize assistant reasoning before history truncation
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes WebChat/history projection leaking raw assistant reasoning scaffolding when a stored assistant message contains malformed or unclosed thinking tags and is reloaded/truncated.
- Routes assistant display text through the existing assistant-visible `history` sanitizer before applying the chat-history text cap.
- Treats `<final>` and `<final/>` as visible-answer boundaries for strict unclosed reasoning recovery, while recursively sanitizing any later reasoning tags.
- Preserves exact tool payload text; tool outputs that happen to contain reasoning-like XML are not rewritten.

Why does this matter now?

- #90692 reports completed assistant replies later reappearing with visible `<thinking>` content and `...(truncated)...` after a history reload/heartbeat.

What is the intended outcome?

- History reloads show only the visible final answer text, with truncation applied after reasoning/final scaffolding is removed.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No config/env surface changes.
- No frontend parser behavior changes.
- No changes to structured tool payload preservation.

What does success look like?

- `chat.history` no longer returns raw thinking/final tags or private reasoning text for the reported malformed transcript shape.
- Existing history truncation, content-block projection, and tool-payload preservation continue to work.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` assistant display text path vs. exact tool payload path.
- `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` strict-mode recovery for unclosed reasoning followed by final tags.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90692

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #90692

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer follow-up from the ClawSweeper source-repro review on #90692.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: WebChat `chat.history` reload/projection sanitizes assistant reasoning/final scaffolding before truncating history text, preventing raw `<thinking>` tags or private reasoning text from appearing after reload.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node 24.15.0; actual OpenClaw gateway projection module loaded through `tsx` and run against a transcript-shaped assistant message.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... import { projectChatDisplayMessages } from './src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts'; ... EOF`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied console output from the command above:

```json
{
  "projectedText": "\nVisible answer after reasoning. Visible answer after reasoning. Visible answer after reasoning.\n...(truncated)...",
  "hasThinkingTag": false,
  "hasFinalTag": false,
  "hasPrivateReasoning": false,
  "hasTruncationMarker": true
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The projected history text keeps visible answer content and the truncation marker, while `hasThinkingTag`, `hasFinalTag`, and `hasPrivateReasoning` are all `false`.
- What was not tested: A live Windows WebChat heartbeat with Google Gemini 3.5 Flash was not reproduced end-to-end.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Crabbox/Testbox changed gate could not run because no usable `crabbox` binary was available in PATH or at `../crabbox/bin/crabbox`; focused local gateway/shared/UI checks, oxlint, tsgo, and autoreview were run instead.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #90692 and the ClawSweeper source-repro comment identify current main truncating assistant text after directive-only stripping in `chat-display-projection`, which can expose malformed reasoning tags on history reload.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... projectChatDisplayMessages(...) ... EOF`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts -t "sanitizeChatHistoryMessages" --reporter=dot`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t "chat.history" --reporter=dot`
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui.config.ts ui/src/ui/format.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Shared assistant-visible sanitizer tests for unclosed reasoning followed by `<final>` and `<final/>`, including later nested reasoning tags after the recovered final boundary.
- Gateway projection tests for assistant `text`, assistant text content blocks, truncation after sanitization, and exact tool payload preservation.
- Gateway `chat.history` RPC test for transcript-backed reload projection with `maxChars`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Source-level behavior: assistant history text was truncated after directive-only stripping, leaving malformed reasoning/final scaffolding available to the UI on reload.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A. Focused regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Assistant history display hides reasoning/final scaffolding before truncation.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, security-relevant display sanitization changed to reduce reasoning leakage in history projection. No auth/secrets/network/tool execution behavior changed.

What is the highest-risk area?

Over-sanitizing non-assistant or tool payload text that should remain exact.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new sanitizer is only applied to assistant display text paths and mixed assistant text projection; exact tool payload branches continue to use the previous directive-only path and have regression coverage.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer and ClawSweeper review.

What review or CI state should maintainers know about?

Local focused checks, oxlint, core tsgo, and autoreview are clean. Crabbox/Testbox changed gate is not available in this environment because the Crabbox binary is missing.

## Summary

- supersedes #90913 with a branch/title/body that reflect the Apple Review demo work
- keeps the iOS App Store release prep on this branch, including iPhone-only submission metadata
- adds an Apple Review offline demo mode activated by setup code `APPLE-REVIEW-DEMO`
- accepts the demo setup code from QR scan, Settings, the legacy manual entry view, and the first-run Connect Gateway -> Set Up Manually flow

## App Review Flow

Apple reviewers can open the app, tap **Set Up Manually** on **Connect Gateway**, paste `APPLE-REVIEW-DEMO` in **Setup Code**, then tap **Apply Setup Code**. The app enters a local offline demo mode and does not require a running Gateway.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:
  Local OpenClaw source worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-90162-discord-outbound`, branch `fix/90162-discord-outbound-adapter`, using deterministic runtime/source tests. Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-selection.test.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`

  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts`

  `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

  `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.ts`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).
